title: Atalantia henryi
intro: Atalantia henryi, also known as the Henry's atalantia, is a species of plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia and known for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Atalantia henryi include limonoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the formulation of pharmaceutical products and dietary supplements.
taxonomy: Atalantia henryi is classified under the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Rutaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as citrus or rue family. It belongs to the order Sapindales, and the family Rutaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, including the subfamily Aurantioideae. Atalantia henryi is a species of flowering plant native to southern China and Southeast Asia. The species is also sometimes classified under the genus Citropsis, which is often considered a synonym for Atalantia. However, the exact classification may vary depending on the source and the latest taxonomic research.
uses: Atalantia henryi has been traditionally used in folk medicine in various parts of the world. Its leaves and fruits are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is also used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and indigestion.
